name: Checkerboard Cookies
recipeInstructions:
1. Cream together the butter and sugar until light and fluffy.
2. Add the vanilla extract to the mixture and stir well.
3. Gradually add the flour and salt, blending until a smooth dough forms.
4. Divide the dough in half.
5. To one half of the dough, add cocoa powder and mix thoroughly.
6. Roll the remaining dough into 1/4-inch thick cylinders, each about 12 inches long.
7. Arrange the cylinders alternating between chocolate and vanilla, creating six layers total.
8. Press the layers together firmly.
9. Wrap the dough in plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least half an hour.
10. Preheat your oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
11. Cut the chilled dough into rounds about 1/3 inch thick using a sharp knife.
12. Place the rounds approximately one inch apart on a parchment-covered baking sheet.
13. Bake for about 10 minutes, or until lightly golden brown.
Yield: Approximately 4 dozen cookies
